mergeRationale: ClosedPoolError is not a subclass of any exception the adapter already catches, so it genuinely leaked; the new except wraps exactly the reported failure as ConnectionError, consistent with existing mappings. This is the fix upstream merged in #2674.

requiredFindings.summary: Confirm ClosedPoolError is not caught by any existing except in send (not a subclass of ProtocolError/OSError/MaxRetryError/_ProxyError/_SSLError/ReadTimeoutError), so it really did leak.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: Because it slips past every existing handler, the new except is needed and correctly targets the reported leak.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: No change needed. Optionally confirm by checking the urllib3 exception hierarchy.

requiredFindings.summary: Confirm the new except is not shadowed by an earlier, broader except (ClosedPoolError has no inheritance relation to ProtocolError/OSError/MaxRetryError), so it is not dead code.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: Ordering of except clauses matters; since ClosedPoolError is unrelated to the earlier handlers, the mapping actually fires.

requiredFindings.summary: Confirm the added test genuinely raises a ClosedPoolError and asserts callers now see ConnectionError, rather than a vacuous test.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: It reproduces the exact leak and asserts the new mapping, so it locks in the behavior change.

optionalFindings.summary: A reviewer may note that other urllib3 exceptions (e.g. DecodeError) could also be wrapped, but that is a separate follow-up, not a blocker for this scoped fix.

disallowedConclusions: Request changes because the patch does not also wrap DecodeError / TimeoutError / other urllib3 exceptions.
The exception boundary is incomplete, so this PR must be expanded before merging.
Reject because the test only covers ClosedPoolError and not the other urllib3 exception types.
